response:
To find the **greatest common divisor (GCD)** of the set $\{-125, -60, -170, -85, 80\}$, we first note that the GCD of a set of integers is the **largest positive integer** that divides all of them without leaving a remainder.

---

### Step 1: Take the absolute values of the numbers

Since the GCD is unaffected by the sign of the numbers, we can work with the absolute values:

$$
\{125, 60, 170, 85, 80\}
$$

---

### Step 2: Use the Euclidean algorithm

We compute the GCD of the numbers step by step.

#### Step 2.1: GCD of 125 and 60

$$
\begin{align*}
125 &= 2 \cdot 60 + 5 \\
60 &= 12 \cdot 5 + 0 \\
\Rightarrow \text{GCD}(125, 60) &= 5
\end{align*}
$$

#### Step 2.2: GCD of 5 and 170

$$
\begin{align*}
170 &= 34 \cdot 5 + 0 \\
\Rightarrow \text{GCD}(5, 170) &= 5
\end{align*}
$$

#### Step 2.3: GCD of 5 and 85

$$
\begin{align*}
85 &= 17 \cdot 5 + 0 \\
\Rightarrow \text{GCD}(5, 85) &= 5
\end{align*}
$$

#### Step 2.4: GCD of 5 and 80

$$
\begin{align*}
80 &= 16 \cdot 5 + 0 \\
\Rightarrow \text{GCD}(5, 80) &= 5
\end{align*}
$$

---

### Final Answer:

$$
\boxed{5}
$$
